verses.1: Now all the tax collectors and sinners were coming near Jesus to listen to Him.
verses.2: And both the Pharisees and the scribes began to complain, saying, “This man receives sinners and eats with them.”
verses.3: And so He told them this parable, saying,
verses.4: “What man among you, if he has a hundred sheep and has lost one of them, does not leave the other ninety-nine in the open pasture and go after the one that is lost, until he finds it?
verses.5: And when he has found it, he puts it on his shoulders, rejoicing.
verses.6: And when he comes home, he calls together his friends and his neighbors, saying to them, ‘Rejoice with me, because I have found my sheep that was lost!’
verses.7: I tell you that in the same way, there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous people who have no need of repentance.
verses.8: “Or what woman, if she has ten silver coins and loses one coin, does not light a lamp and sweep the house and search carefully until she finds it?
verses.9: And when she has found it, she calls together her friends and neighbors, saying, ‘Rejoice with me, because I have found the coin which I had lost!’
verses.10: In the same way, I tell you, there is joy in the presence of the angels of God over one sinner who repents.”
verses.11: And He said, “A man had two sons.
verses.12: The younger of them said to his father, ‘Father, give me the share of the estate that is coming to me.’ And so he divided his wealth between them.
verses.13: And not many days later, the younger son gathered everything together and went on a journey to a distant country, and there he squandered his estate in wild living.
verses.14: Now when he had spent everything, a severe famine occurred in that country, and he began doing without.
verses.15: So he went and hired himself out to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him into his fields to feed pigs.
verses.16: And he longed to have his fill of the carob pods that the pigs were eating, and no one was giving him anything.
verses.17: But when he came to his senses, he said, ‘How many of my father’s hired laborers have more than enough bread, but I am dying here from hunger!
verses.18: I will set out and go to my father, and will say to him, “Father, I have sinned against heaven, and in your sight;
verses.19: I am no longer worthy to be called your son; treat me as one of your hired laborers.”’
verses.20: So he set out and came to his father. But when he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt compassion for him, and ran and embraced him and kissed him.
verses.21: And the son said to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against heaven and in your sight; I am no longer worthy to be called your son.’
verses.22: But the father said to his slaves, ‘Quickly bring out the best robe and put it on him, and put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet;
verses.23: and bring the fattened calf, slaughter it, and let’s eat and celebrate;
verses.24: for this son of mine was dead and has come to life again; he was lost and has been found.’ And they began to celebrate.
verses.25: “Now his older son was in the field, and when he came and approached the house, he heard music and dancing.
verses.26: And he summoned one of the servants and began inquiring what these things could be.
verses.27: And he said to him, ‘Your brother has come, and your father has slaughtered the fattened calf because he has received him back safe and sound.’
verses.28: But he became angry and was not willing to go in; and his father came out and began pleading with him.
verses.29: But he answered and said to his father, ‘Look! For so many years I have been serving you and I have never neglected a command of yours; and yet you never gave me a young goat, so that I might celebrate with my friends;
verses.30: but when this son of yours came, who has devoured your wealth with prostitutes, you slaughtered the fattened calf for him.’
verses.31: And he said to him, ‘Son, you have always been with me, and all that is mine is yours.
verses.32: But we had to celebrate and rejoice, because this brother of yours was dead and has begun to live, and was lost and has been found.’”
